title: narcissus pallidulus subsp. lusitanicus
intro: Narcissus pallidulus subsp. lusitanicus, also known as the Portuguese winter aconite, is a bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its white flowers and toxic properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the heart and nervous system, and as an antispasmodic and sedative. The health benefits of Narcissus pallidulus subsp. lusitanicus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and epilepsy, as well as its use in treating heart conditions and as a diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically used in small quantities due to its toxicity, and should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
